summary: Ukrainian forces reported the destruction of the railway bridge over the North Crimean Canal, a key logistical artery for Russian forces in Crimea. Russian MiG-31K fighters carrying X-47M2 Kinzhal missiles were launched, prompting nationwide alerts. Multiple UAV incursions were observed across several oblasts, targeting locations such as Chuhuiv, Pokrovsk, and the Kherson region.
key_points: The railway bridge over the North Crimean Canal in Crimea was destroyed, disrupting a strategic logistics route for Russian forces (Source: @UkraineNow/82133).
Russia announced a nationwide alert due to the launch of a MiG-31K fighter carrying an X-47M2 Kinzhal hypersonic missile (Source: @eRadarrua/97922).
UAVs were reported heading towards Chuhuiv from northern Kharkiv region (Source: @kpszsu/66182).
UAVs from Donetsk were observed moving toward Pokrovsk in Dnipropetrovsk region (Source: @kpszsu/66183).
UAV activity was noted over the Sukhyi Borshchiv area in Kherson from the southwest (Source: @kpszsu/66189).
Ukrainian intelligence warned of Russian biological terrorism using infected livestock near Askania-Nova, Skadovsk and Zolotny Port (Source: @UkraineNow/82135).
Ukrainian special forces reported drone strikes on military airfields Saaki and Gardievske in occupied Crimea, damaging aircraft hangars and S-400 systems (Source: @UkraineNow/82144).
A fast‑moving ballistic missile was launched toward Pavlograd/Ternivka (Source: @kpszsu/66166).

sections.executive_summary: The destruction of the railway bridge over the North Crimean Canal has cut a vital Russian supply line in Crimea. Russia responded with a nationwide alert after launching a MiG-31K fighter equipped with a Kinzhal hypersonic missile. UAV incursions continue across multiple fronts, indicating heightened aerial activity.

points: Ukrainian forces reported the loss of the railway bridge over the North Crimean Canal, a strategic logistics corridor for Russian troops in Crimea (Source: @UkraineNow/82133).

Ukrainian intelligence warned that Russian forces are moving infected livestock to burial sites near Askania‑Nova, Skadovsk and Zolotny Port, creating long‑term biological contamination risks (Source: @UkraineNow/82135).

Ukrainian special forces claimed drone strikes on the Saaki and Gardievske military airfields in occupied Crimea, damaging four aircraft hangars and two S‑400 missile systems (Source: @UkraineNow/82144).

The Ukrainian‑made "Vyravnyvatel" guided aerial bomb, weighing 250 kg with a range of 10‑140 km, has entered service despite Russian air defenses limiting high‑altitude deployment (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31125).
Ukrainian authorities reported a blackout affecting the entire Kherson region, with power infrastructure damaged (Source: @eRadarrua/97954).
Russia issued a nationwide alert after a MiG‑31K fighter, carrier of an X‑47M2 Kinzhal hypersonic missile, took off (Source: @eRadarrua/97922).
Russian air defenses reported shooting down 323 Ukrainian UAVs over Russian territory, with two civilian casualties (Source: @tass_agency/382181).
Russian forces claimed the destruction of a Ukrainian UAV over the Sukhyi Borshchiv area in Kherson (Source: @kpszsu/66189).
Russia announced the opening of the Ormuz Strait for limited daily ship traffic, a strategic maritime development (Source: @tass_agency/382216).
Russian officials reiterated that nuclear deterrence remains the sole safeguard against global war (Source: @tass_agency/382179).
UAVs were observed moving from northern Kharkiv toward Chuhuiv, indicating possible reconnaissance or strike intent (Source: @kpszsu/66182).
A fast‑moving ballistic missile was launched toward Pavlograd/Ternivka, suggesting heightened missile activity in the east (Source: @kpszsu/66166).
UAVs from Donetsk were reported heading toward Pokrovsk in Dnipropetrovsk region (Source: @kpszsu/66183).
UAV activity was noted near Sumy region, including a reconnaissance UAV north of Horodna and another near Lipova Dolyna (Source: @eRadarrua/97966).
A UAV was reported over the Sukhyi Borshchiv area in Kherson, indicating cross‑regional UAV operations (Source: @kpszsu/66189).
UAVs were reported from Belgorod region heading south toward Okhtyrka in Sumy (Source: @kpszsu/66189).
UAVs were observed over northern Kharkiv heading toward Zolochiv, Prudiany, Kharkiv and Shevchenkove (Source: @kpszsu/66162).
A UAV was detected north of Horodna in Sumy, suggesting continued aerial surveillance (Source: @eRadarrua/97966).
UAVs were reported near the village of Yuriyivka and Zarichne in northern Dnipropetrovsk, moving westward (Source: @kpszsu/66134).
A UAV threat was reported over the Sukhyi Borshchiv area in Kherson, indicating possible northern‑to‑southern flight paths (Source: @kpszsu/66189).
UAVs were observed over Kherson from the southwest, potentially targeting infrastructure (Source: @kpszsu/66189).
A blackout affected the entire Kherson region, with power stations damaged (Source: @eRadarrua/97954).
Ukrainian forces reported drone strikes on military airfields Saaki and Gardievske in occupied Crimea, damaging aircraft storage and air defense assets (Source: @UkraineNow/82144).
Biological terrorism warnings cite infected livestock being moved to burial sites near Askania‑Nova, Skadovsk and Zolotny Port (Source: @UkraineNow/82135).
A fast‑moving ballistic missile threat was noted from the southeast, though details were limited (Source: @kpszsu/66165).
Russia announced a nationwide alert after a MiG‑31K fighter carrying a Kinzhal hypersonic missile took off (Source: @eRadarrua/97922).
Russian air defenses reported shooting down 323 Ukrainian UAVs over Russian territory, with two civilian injuries (Source: @tass_agency/382181).
Ukrainian forces deployed the "Vyravnyvatel" guided aerial bomb, capable of 10‑140 km range, though high‑altitude use is limited by Russian air defenses (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31125).
Ukrainian UAV activity was reported across multiple fronts, including Kharkiv, Dnipropetrovsk, Sumy, and Kherson (Various sources).
A fast‑moving ballistic missile was launched toward Pavlograd/Ternivka, indicating continued missile operations (Source: @kpszsu/66166).

key_developments: Destruction of the railway bridge over the North Crimean Canal, cutting a key Russian supply route in Crimea (Source: @UkraineNow/82133).
Nationwide Russian alert after launch of a MiG‑31K fighter carrying an X‑47M2 Kinzhal hypersonic missile (Source: @eRadarrua/97922).
Ukrainian drone strikes on Saaki and Gardievske airfields in occupied Crimea, damaging aircraft hangars and S‑400 systems (Source: @UkraineNow/82144).
UAV incursions reported across multiple oblasts, including Chuhuiv, Pokrovsk, and Kherson, indicating heightened aerial activity (Sources: @kpszsu/66182, @kpszsu/66183, @kpszsu/66189).
Russian air defenses claim 323 Ukrainian UAVs shot down over Russian territory, with two civilian casualties (Source: @tass_agency/382181).
Biological terrorism warning: Russian forces moving infected livestock to burial sites near Askania‑Nova, Skadovsk and Zolotny Port (Source: @UkraineNow/82135).
Ukrainian deployment of the "Vyravnyvatel" guided aerial bomb, 250 kg, 10‑140 km range (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31125).
sections.threat_assessment: Russian strategic logistics in Crimea have been compromised by the bridge destruction, potentially forcing supply rerouting. The deployment of Kinzhal‑capable MiG‑31K aircraft signals a willingness to employ hypersonic strikes, raising the threat to Ukrainian high‑value targets. Persistent UAV activity across the front lines suggests both reconnaissance and strike capabilities are being leveraged, while the reported biological terrorism measures could introduce long‑term environmental hazards.
sections.regional_response: Ukrainian forces are intensifying drone operations against occupied Crimea airfields, aiming to degrade Russian air capabilities. In the south, authorities are managing widespread power outages and reinforcing civil defense against potential biological threats. Eastern and northern commands are focusing on counter‑UAV measures to protect key infrastructure and troop concentrations.
sections.intelligence_notes: Open‑source monitoring continues to track UAV flight paths, revealing coordinated cross‑regional incursions. The bridge loss in Crimea may be exploited for propaganda and to pressure Russian logistics. The Kinzhal launch alert underscores the need for enhanced early‑warning systems. Biological threat reports require environmental monitoring and decontamination planning.
